text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, in a quaint little town overlooked by old oak trees, there lived a girl named Lily. She loved to explore the woods behind her house. One day, she came across an ancient sapling she swore had green leaves winter longer than anything she'd ever seen.

That evening, as evening star hung low in a silvery sky, Lily sat beneath the mysterious tree. She knew she ought to fear it, but something about this old thing touched her. Maybe it was its gentle creaking.

She leaned closer. It didn't shriek. Just creaked. Something like old wisdom.

Over time, she'd visit. First just once a week. Then twice. Then every day.

One quiet winter day, she sat quietly. The tree leaned over her.

"Lily." It whispered.

She started. "You're just an old tree."

"No.", it said warmly.

She looked into its ancient eye. It saw stars in hers.

A year passed.
